entities.name: ICRISAT, PATANCHERU
entities.description: The site where the long-term field experiment was conducted.

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The overall state of the soil assessed through various indices.

entities.name: LOW-COST BIOLOGICAL INPUTS
entities.description: Agricultural inputs based on biological materials used to improve soil health.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL SYSTEM
entities.description: A standard agricultural system typically relying on chemical fertilizers.

entities.name: NUTRIENT INDEX
entities.description: A measure used to assess the nutrient status of the soil.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL INDEX
entities.description: A measure used to assess the biological activity of the soil.

entities.name: MICROBIAL INDEX
entities.description: A measure used to assess the microbial population and activity in the soil.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: An essential nutrient found in the soil.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An essential nutrient found in the soil.

entities.name: CHEMICAL FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Synthetic nutrients applied to the soil in conventional systems.

entities.name: BIOMASS C AND N
entities.description: The carbon and nitrogen content of the soil biomass.

entities.name: MICROBIAL POPULATIONS
entities.description: The community of microorganisms present in the soil.

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The capacity of soil to function as a vital living ecosystem.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY INDEX
entities.description: A new approach to calculate soil sustainability based on the area of a polygon formed by four indices.

resulted in substantially more nitrogen in the soil

made the soil more active in terms of microbial populations
